non_claims: No model was loaded, downloaded, or benchmarked.
Transport compatibility, semantic quality, and product acceptance were not established.
No host integration, implementation, commit, or push was performed.

allowed: host-owned adapters -> host transport, lifecycle, cancellation, persistence, and UI
forbidden: LabKit core -> benchmark or report tooling

direct.boundary: Interchange DTOs only; not host persistence models.
Host owns business meaning and task-specific policy.
Normalization remains separately reported and never counts as semantic repair.
Host wraps the inner schema in the provider response format and owns schema versioning.
Use only the tested JSON Schema keyword subset or pair with a complete host validator.
Host observes state and executes decisions; package policy remains side-effect free.
Relocate into the stable core namespace before product dependency.
Host owns cumulative retry budget, cancellation, and product safety.

adapt.reason: Current seam is synchronous, text-only, structured-JSON-only, OpenAI-compatible-only, parallel-one, fixed-context, and owns load/cleanup around an initially empty state.
adapt.adapter: Host-native async/cancellable structured transport and lifecycle port.

adapt.reason: Lab quality heuristics do not define universal product behavior.
adapt.adapter: Task policy selects checks and maps warning/fail outcomes to host fallback and review.

adapt.reason: Fake-first privacy-safe clients model transport contracts; generation sends identity hashes rather than production request bodies.
adapt.adapter: Host transport materializes real bodies and maps typed errors.

adapt.reason: Identity vocabulary is reusable; lab candidate and recommendation status is not runtime truth.
Contracts describe evidence shapes but do not prove cache reuse or choose product context.
adapt.adapter: Host context planner maps current, neighbor, chunk, compact-memory, and full-recording policies.

adapt.reason: Payload and outcome concepts are reusable, but the controller combines experiment policy and lifecycle.
adapt.adapter: Small host vision transport that reports transport, raw parse, schema, semantic grounding, and product decision separately.

adapt.reason: LabKit builders return inner schemas while host providers accept the full response format.

exclude.reason: Experiment planning, execution matrices, artifact writing, reporting, and resume behavior are not request-core responsibilities.
Experiment-specific allowlists, fixtures, private captures, preflights, global-zero assumptions, and launch accounting.
Would duplicate and bypass host networking, queue, cancellation, lifecycle ownership, and error handling.
Lab evidence and admission records are not dynamic product configuration.
Probes, acquisition, live smoke, config, metrics, report, and cache-plan generation are experiment scaffolding; only context-fit merits relocation.
Owner-only experiment diagnostics have different retention and privacy obligations from product telemetry.
Benchmark report consumption is not a production request execution port.

adapter_seams.name: host_request_mapper
adapter_seams.responsibility: Map a host operation to RequestEnvelope, ResponseContract, and ExecutionOptions without leaking persistence types into LabKit.

adapter_seams.name: structured_transport_port
adapter_seams.responsibility: Async/cancellable transport for messages, strict response format, image parts, output budget, timeout, untouched response surface, finish reason, usage, and typed errors.

adapter_seams.name: lifecycle_port
adapter_seams.responsibility: Observe runtime, apply pure lifecycle decisions, return an ownership handle, and release only owned instances.

adapter_seams.name: validation_pipeline
adapter_seams.responsibility: Separate transport, raw parse, optional bounded normalization, schema/ID validation, semantic review, and host product decision.

adapter_seams.name: context_planner
adapter_seams.responsibility: Host selects current-only, boundary-neighbor, adjacent-chunk, compact-memory, or full-recording context; LabKit evaluates fit and evidence.

adapter_seams.name: registry_adapter
adapter_seams.responsibility: Map host configuration to stable model identity/capability records without auto-promoting lab recommendations.

packaging_risks.risk: The wheel exposes repository layout as three package trees: lmstudio_labkit, libs/lmstudio_managed, and tools/lmstudio_lab.
The top-level facade eagerly exports benchmark, live, strict-vision, artifact, and report symbols alongside request DTOs.
The alpha package does not establish a stable semantic-version compatibility surface.
All consumers receive LM Studio SDK, Pillow, and YAML dependencies even when importing standard-library-only core contracts.
Pure contracts are split across lmstudio_labkit, libs, and tools namespaces.
ResponseContract combines universal structure with lab-specific semantic policy.
The custom schema validator supports a tested subset, not complete JSON Schema semantics.
Strict vision combines request payload, fixtures, lifecycle, forensics, grounding, and launch control in one large module.
Copy-based integration would create split ownership and drift between package and host source copies.

smallest_extraction_strategy: Create a stable lmstudio_labkit.core surface for request/result DTOs, structural response contracts, JSON normalization, tested schema builders, structural validators, lifecycle state/policy, context fit, and bounded output-budget decisions.
Create lmstudio_labkit.ports containing protocols only; host code implements transport, lifecycle execution, cancellation, image materialization, registry mapping, and persistence.
Keep benchmark, strict-vision controllers, artifacts, reports, datasets, forensics, and live runners in a lab-only namespace or optional extra.
Make the host application depend on the package contract kernel and prohibit all host imports, host field names, host prompts, and host persistence types inside LabKit.
Protect the direction with import-boundary tests and fake-adapter compatibility tests; reserve live integration for a later explicit gate.
evidence: read-only host application structured text and vision source inspection
